atDirectives.name: @tailwind
atDirectives.description: Use the `@tailwind` directive to insert Tailwind's `base`, `components`, `utilities` and `screens` styles into your CSS.

atDirectives.name: @apply
atDirectives.description: Use the `@apply` directive to inline any existing utility classes into your own custom CSS.

atDirectives.name: @custom-variant
atDirectives.description: This is a custom directive (not part of standard Tailwind) used for defining pseudo-class variants like `dark`, `hover`, etc. in some extended configurations or plugins.

atDirectives.name: @plugin
atDirectives.description: A non-standard directive often used in custom build systems or preprocessors to import Tailwind-compatible plugins directly in CSS files. Not supported by default.

atDirectives.name: @source
atDirectives.description: This directive is not part of Tailwind or PostCSS, and appears to be used for including external JavaScript/TypeScript files related to themes or components.

atDirectives.name: @config
atDirectives.description: A placeholder directive that may indicate where the Tailwind configuration file is located. Not recognized by Tailwind itself.
